verbGlossary.PROFILES: {source} establishes a characteristic risk profile for {target} category (e.g., project type profiles the overall risk category distribution for hospitals vs. warehouses)
verbGlossary.CALIBRATES: {source} adjusts the thresholds within {target} scoring framework (e.g., project type calibrates risk matrix severity levels for healthcare vs. commercial)
verbGlossary.SCALES: {source} proportionally increases the magnitude of {target} or reserve (e.g., total value band scales financial risk exposure)
verbGlossary.ELEVATES: {source} raises the likelihood or severity of {target} category (e.g., seismic design category elevates geotechnical risk, fast-track elevates schedule risk)
verbGlossary.DETERMINES: {source} dictates how {target} allocation is structured between parties (e.g., delivery method determines risk allocation between owner and contractor)
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source} bonding attribute activates the requirement for {target} (e.g., bonding requirement triggers surety bond procurement)
verbGlossary.FUNDS: {source} financial attribute provides monetary backing for {target} reserve (e.g., contingency budget funds the contingency reserve)
verbGlossary.INFLUENCES: {source} attribute affects the probability or nature of {target} category (e.g., new vs. existing construction influences design risk due to unknown conditions)
verbGlossary.DRIVES: {source} performance metric causes movement in {target} consumption rate (e.g., cost growth drives contingency burn rate)
verbGlossary.ERODES: {source} schedule variance metric reduces {target} buffer (e.g., schedule growth erodes schedule float)
verbGlossary.SIGNALS: {source} earned value metric provides an early warning indicator for {target} category (e.g., SPI below 1.0 signals schedule risk)
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED AGAINST: {source}-level rate metric is compared to {target} metric for performance evaluation (e.g., project change order rate benchmarked against risk change order rate)
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS: {source} code attribute imposes regulatory limits that shape {target} category (e.g., building code edition constrains regulatory and permit risk)
verbGlossary.MITIGATES: {source} resilience attribute reduces the exposure to {target} category (e.g., resilience requirements mitigate environmental risk through design standards)
verbGlossary.TRACKED IN: {source} outcome is documented and monitored through {target} -- the completeness and currency of {target} reflects the maturity of {source} practice
verbGlossary.QUANTIFIED BY: {source} outcome is measured in financial terms through {target} calculation -- this converts qualitative risk assessment into monetary impact
verbGlossary.ACTIONED THROUGH: {source} outcome is achieved by executing {target} strategies -- the type and effectiveness of {target} determines how much {source} is actually realized
